problem_24.natural_language: A finite group of prime order is cyclic.
problem_24.lean:
theorem isCyclic_of_prime_card_fintype {G : Type*} [Group G] [Fintype G] (h : Nat.Prime (Fintype.card G)) :
    IsCyclic G
problem_24.natural_language_proof:
Proof: let `|G| = p` with `p` prime. Pick `g` not equal to `1`. Consider the subgroup generated by
`g`; its
cardinality divides `|G|` (Lagrange). It is not `1` since it contains `g`, so its order is a
divisor of `p` different from `1`, hence it must be `p`. Therefore this subgroup is all of `G`,
so `g` generates `G` and `G` is cyclic.
problem_24.lean_proof:
by
  let p := Fintype.card G
  have h_card : Nat.card G = p := by rw [Nat.card_eq_fintype_card]
  have hp : Fact p.Prime := by apply Fact.mk; simpa [p] using h
  exact isCyclic_of_prime_card h_card
